current.summary: Designed and wrote the Agent Content Provenance (ACP) specification — portable meta.yaml sidecar format for AI coding tool content provenance with dual hashes, Sigstore/SSH signing, and lineage tracking.

activeWork.description: ACP spec v0.1.0 draft complete. Went through full design cycle: brainstorm → content type mapping → adversarial panels (3 rounds) → external review (Claude + Gemini) → spec writing using spec-writing skill.
activeWork.progress: ACP spec draft complete at docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md. Design doc with full decision history at docs/spec/skills/provenance.md. All 12 discussion points resolved or scoped out. Two rounds of external review feedback incorporated. One remaining task: generate inline test vectors for Appendix B.

git.lastCommit: 3b34379 handoff: CDR execution complete, content-based classification gap identified

recentDecisions: Named the spec 'Agent Content Provenance (ACP)' — tool-agnostic, not syllago-branded
One provenance model for all content types — no executable vs instructional tiers. LLM is the execution engine; anything in context can cause execution.
Universal meta.yaml sidecar (not type-specific names like SKILL.meta.yaml) — type field inside is sufficient
Separate content_hash and meta_hash — content edits don't break metadata integrity and vice versa, but meta_hash INCLUDES content_hash to prevent mix-and-match attacks
Sigstore (OIDC) as primary signing method, SSH as fallback — platform-agnostic, not GitHub-specific. CI/CD Action = Let's Encrypt model for adoption.
JCS (RFC 8785) only for meta_hash, NOT for content files — raw bytes for all content hashing to reduce implementer burden
derived_from is a list (supports compose with multiple parents), all entries must use same relation type
Integer versioning (not semver), version resets to 1 on fork
Loadouts removed from spec (syllago-specific) — spec covers 6 types: skill, agent, hook, rule, mcp, command
Revocation is registry/distribution concern, not provenance spec — full design captured in roadmap for syllago implementation
Single .syllago/manifest.yaml for syllago bookkeeping (not per-item .syllago.yaml) — manifest is local, never travels
Collection-level provenance deferred — per-item meta.yaml for v0.1, collection approach needs more design
nextSteps: Generate inline test vectors for Appendix B — actual byte sequences and expected SHA-256 hashes for all 15 test cases
Run spec audit using /spec-writing audit workflow to catch any remaining issues
Commit all spec files (acp-spec.md, provenance.md, llm-feedback.md)
Consider moving acp-spec.md to its own top-level directory (docs/spec/acp/) since it covers all content types, not just skills
Plan syllago implementation: meta.yaml generation in publish flow, hash verification in install flow, Sigstore integration
Build reference implementation for content hash algorithm with the 15 test vectors
Design the CI/CD GitHub Action and GitLab CI template for automatic signing

context.notes:
KEY FILES:

1. docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md — THE SPEC. ACP v0.1.0 draft. Proper RFC 2119 language, conformance classes, security considerations.

2. docs/spec/skills/provenance.md — DESIGN DOC. Full decision history with rationale for every choice. All 12 discussion points (D1-D12) documented with outcomes. Panel results preserved.

3. docs/spec/skills/llm-feedback.md — EXTERNAL REVIEW. Feedback from Claude and Gemini on earlier draft. All issues addressed: derived_from list, meta_hash binding, namespace guidance, section numbering, test vectors, source format.

ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS:
- meta_hash includes content_hash (prevents mix-and-match attack where legitimate metadata paired with malicious content)
- JCS only for meta_hash serialization, NOT for content file hashing (reduces implementer burden — consumers only need SHA-256)
- Sigstore OIDC is platform-agnostic (GitHub, GitLab, CircleCI, Buildkite, Forgejo) — trust anchor is OIDC protocol, not any platform
- Revocation system designed (revocations.json in registry index, signed entries, shrink detection) but scoped to registry layer, not provenance spec

ROADMAP ITEMS (in provenance.md):
- Revocation system (registry layer) — panel-designed, ready for implementation
- Collection-level provenance — single meta.yaml for repos with many items
- Provenance fingerprint — 12-char hash in content frontmatter for orphan reconnection
- Key-level revocation — revoke signing key, flag all content signed by it
- Push-based revocation notifications

previousSessions.summary: Design provenance and attestation model for syllago content types — authorship, versioning, source binding, derivation lineage, content hashing.
completedWork: Extensive prior research and adversarial review. Prior panel recommended 8-field minimum sidecar convention. Created initial handoff with full context from prior work.
nextSteps: Map content types to provenance needs
